source.title: Chronic administration of clozapine alleviates reversal-learning impairment in isolation-reared rats

confidence_rationale: Behavioural pharmacology study; demonstrates that post-weaning isolation rearing specifically impairs reversal learning (new-strategy acquisition phase) but not initial acquisition learning in rats. This is the monostrategy signature: the isolation-reared animal learns one strategy but cannot update it. Source quality moderate (small n, pharmacological intervention focus); mapping fidelity good for the specific claim that impoverished early exploration -> rigid behavioral repertoire.
failure_signatures: Isolation rearing impairs social experience as well as environmental exploration; the specific contribution of motor/sensory exploration deprivation vs. social deprivation cannot be separated.
Clozapine remediation suggests the effect is dopaminergic/prefrontal, but this does not establish the hippocampal trajectory store as the locus of the deficit.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Isolation-reared rats perform significantly worse than socially-reared rats on reversal learning (new-strategy acquisition), but not on initial discrimination learning; this deficit is partially reversed by clozapine.
mapping.ree_translation: This is the developmental monostrategy result: insufficient early exploration (social + environmental impoverishment) produces animals that can learn one strategy but cannot acquire a new strategy when contingencies change. In REE terms, the behavioral option store was not populated during the developmental window, so when a new context demands a different trajectory, retrieval fails and the animal repeats the previously-reinforced pattern.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Isolation rearing is a global deprivation; the mechanism of the flexibility deficit (reduced hippocampal place field diversity? PFC dopamine depletion?) is not established. Transfer risk: the schizophrenia model framing is context-specific.
mapping.source_context: Sprague-Dawley rats; post-weaning isolation rearing; rotating T-maze reversal learning task; Behavioural Pharmacology 2007.

confidence_components.notes: Good behavioral result; mechanism not resolved; moderate transfer risk from schizophrenia model to general developmental bootstrapping claim.
